title: Internet Explorer market share correlates with the US murder rate (r = 0.923)
description: Internet Explorer's browser market share closely tracked the US murder rate from 2006–2011 (r = 0.923). Both declined independently over the same period. Used as a reductio-ad-absurdum argument against drawing policy conclusions from aggregate trend data without controls, and as a memorable example of why declining macroeconomic indicators often appear correlated with one another.
